title: Iberidella pygmaea
taxonomy: Iberidella pygmaea belongs to the family Iridaceae. It is a species of flowering plant commonly known as the Pygmy Iris. Iberidella pygmaea is native to South Africa. The plant is a bulbous perennial that grows in a variety of habitats. It is known for its small, delicate flowers in shades of purple and white.

intro: Iberidella pygmaea, also known as pygmy grape-hyacinth, is a small, bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the family Iridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Iberidella pygmaea include isoflavones, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
